title: Moiras Nudes

jokes.text: Well, how's that for timing! We both stepped out exactly together.
jokes.setup: Two people exit a building at the same time
jokes.punchline: Johnny's overly excited reaction to a mundane coincidence
jokes.explanation: Sets up Johnny's tendency to overexplain mundane things, classic character comedy but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Did you see that? We exited at the exact same time! What are the odds? This is incredible. This is fate. This is the most extraordinary thing that's ever happened to me.

jokes.text: Oh my God, is that David?
jokes.setup: Alexis sees someone in a car
jokes.punchline: Her shock at David having transportation
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character moment showing family's low expectations for David

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that... David? In a car? David, you're driving?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, Alexis, I'm driving. It's a car. I operate it.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know what a car is. I'm just shocked that you know how to drive one.

jokes.text: Do you think I'd allow someone to buy that car for me?
jokes.setup: Family assumes David got a nice car
jokes.punchline: David's indignation at the suggestion, despite being broke
jokes.explanation: Classic David snobbery even when poor, good character integration

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't get a nice car. I got a car. It's functional. It has wheels and an engine, and it does what a car is supposed to do.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, it's a very nice car, David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not nice! It's beige! It's aggressively beige. And the interior smells like a wet dog that's been left in the sun.

jokes.text: Um, very unclear on the 'we' part of that sentence.
jokes.setup: Alexis assumes she can use David's car
jokes.punchline: David's dry clarification of ownership
jokes.explanation: Perfect David delivery, excellent economy and structure

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Can I borrow your car?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not my car, Alexis. It's the family car.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, so I can borrow it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No. It's the family car, which means it belongs to all of us, which means it belongs to none of us.

jokes.text: There's a button missing on your jacket. What? Oh ew, you poor thing.
jokes.setup: Family discussing their improving fortunes
jokes.punchline: Alexis notices Johnny's shabby appearance
jokes.explanation: Visual detail reinforcing their financial struggles

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, I have to say, you're looking a little... rough around the edges these days.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, Alexis, when you're rebuilding a business empire from the ground up, grooming takes a backseat.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I get that, but you look like you're about to ask me for spare change.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's actually not far off from where we started, so I'll take it as progress.

jokes.text: We have a limit as to how much you can charge without actually making any effort to pay.
jokes.setup: Johnny tries to add to their tab
jokes.punchline: Twyla's polite but pointed observation about their payment habits
jokes.explanation: Twyla's sweet but firm character coming through, good economy

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Could you just add that to our tab?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Of course, Mr. Rose. Although, I should mention your tab is now at $4,000.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, we'll pay it eventually.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm sure you will. In about... never.

jokes.text: I have dated enough Wall Street losers to know that 'making things liquid' is not a good thing!
jokes.setup: Johnny uses financial euphemisms
jokes.punchline: Alexis's unexpected financial literacy from dating experience
jokes.explanation: Great character reveal showing Alexis is more observant than she seems

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We need to start thinking about our liquid assets and diversifying our portfolio.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, finally! I've been diversifying my portfolio since I was sixteen.

jokes.text: I just had to use my unemployment check this month to pay for all the raw milk that 'we' ordered.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining their financial situation
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive emphasis on Moira's expensive tastes
jokes.explanation: Shows marital dynamics and class clash humor

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, we've lost everything. Our entire fortune is gone.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Not everything, darling. I still have my wigs.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, Moira. Your wigs. The $47,000 collection of wigs.

jokes.text: No, I can't type, and I don't care to relive my days as a secretary.
jokes.setup: Stevie offers to let Moira use computer alone
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic refusal to do basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira refusing menial work, good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I couldn't possibly. I don't know how to use a computer. I've never touched one in my life.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I've touched one, but not in a way that would be helpful to anyone.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I once sat on one at a gala in Monaco. Does that count?

jokes.text: The top eleven photographs of Moira Rose with future murder suspects.
jokes.setup: Looking for compromising photos of Moira
jokes.punchline: Internet headline about her connections to criminals
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific, perfect dark comedy about celebrity culture

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why are you looking for compromising photos of my mother?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not looking for compromising photos. I'm just seeing what comes up when you search her name online.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, there's this headline: 'Local Woman's Suspicious Connections to Multiple Criminal Enterprises.'

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's just from her time in the entertainment industry.

jokes.text: That was the one time he let me play with his hair. It was a sunny aft... you know, I shouldn't have to explain myself.
jokes.setup: Stevie asks about Phil Spector photo
jokes.punchline: Moira's innocent but tone-deaf reminiscence
jokes.explanation: Moira's obliviousness to inappropriate associations, great cringe

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, Phil Spector. Yes, we met at a charity gala in the '80s. He was very charming, very talented. We danced, had cocktails. I had no idea about any of that other business. I just remember thinking he had wonderful hands for a murderer— I mean, for a musician.

jokes.text: I mean, I've always had clean, radiant skin, but I'm an anomaly.
jokes.setup: David has a pimple
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual self-aggrandizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis vanity, good character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know, I've never had a pimple in my life. I think it's because I've always had really good skincare, but also just like, really good genes. My dermatologist in New York said I had the skin of a teenager, and I was like, 'Well, I am a teenager,' but he was like, 'No, like a really, really good-looking teenager.'

jokes.text: I don't even wear good socks in here!
jokes.setup: David drops face mask on motel floor
jokes.punchline: His specific complaint about motel hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect David fastidiousness, good specificity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Did that just fall on the floor? This floor hasn't been cleaned since the Carter administration. I'm not putting that back on my face. I'd probably get some sort of flesh-eating disease.

jokes.text: See David, this is what happens when you wash your face with a bar of motel hand soap!
jokes.setup: David has a pimple
jokes.punchline: Alexis's immediate diagnosis involving their living situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's beauty expertise and their reduced circumstances

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, that's from living in a motel room. You're breaking out from the stress of it all.

jokes.text: If someone has to find naked pictures of me, better you than a stranger in a storm cellar!
jokes.setup: Moira needs help finding nude photos
jokes.punchline: Her oddly specific and dark comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird Moira logic, perfect absurdist escalation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I need help finding some nude photos of myself.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why would you need help finding photos of yourself?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Because they're not of me, they're of someone who looks like me, and I need to find them before they surface.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's like looking for a needle in a haystack, except the needle is my body and the haystack is the entire internet.

jokes.text: Um, are you sure you're not thinking of mine?
jokes.setup: Moira mentions nude photos online
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually suggests her own exist
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis oversharing, great economy and character truth

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: There are nude photos of me online.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, there are nude photos of me online too, so...

jokes.text: Three words I thought I'd only have to type if I was held at gunpoint.
jokes.setup: David searching for his mother's nude photos
jokes.punchline: His dramatic assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good David dramatic overstatement about uncomfortable family moment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not looking for my mother's nude photos. I'm trying to find pictures of her in a context where she's fully clothed and making a sensible life choice.

jokes.text: I'd argue that moment happens daily.
jokes.setup: Moira says they've reached an awkward parent/child moment
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact response about the frequency
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - deadpan acknowledgment of his family's dysfunction

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I think we've reached that awkward parent/child moment.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I think we reached that about fifteen years ago, and we've just been living in it ever since.

jokes.text: Okay, try 'effervescence.' No, I'm sorry, 'small umbrella.' No wait, 'Siamese eels.'
jokes.setup: Searching for Moira's nude photos
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre, pretentious search terms
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Moira vocabulary, escalating weirdness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, what are these search terms? 'Ethereal goddess in repose'? 'Transcendent form suspended in time'?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not going to search for 'naked pictures of me.' That's vulgar.

jokes.text: Can no one find nude photos of me on the Internet?!
jokes.setup: Search for compromising photos fails
jokes.punchline: Moira's frustrated outburst about lack of nude photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect inversion - frustration at NOT finding embarrassing content

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I don't understand. Where are the nudes? Every other semi-relevant public figure has at least one compromising photo circulating on the internet. Where are mine?

jokes.text: My Uncle Ken only has three fingers now, which is too bad, because he's deaf, and he only speaks using sign language, but... He made his choices.
jokes.setup: Twyla mentions debt consequences
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to uncle's injury with dark punchline
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Twyla moment, perfect deadpan delivery of horrible story

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: My uncle lost his arm in a workplace accident, and he couldn't afford the medical bills, so now he has a lot of debt.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: But at least he doesn't have to worry about paying for two sleeves anymore.

jokes.text: No, no, don't worry, because then I would have to pay you back, which is gonna be hard because my entire family is broke, and we can't afford to eat right now, so.
jokes.setup: Ted offers to buy Alexis a smoothie
jokes.punchline: Alexis's oversharing about family poverty
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Alexis's lack of filter

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes, I would love a smoothie. You know, when we were poor, we couldn't afford smoothies. We had to blend our own fruit, which sounds nice, but we didn't have a blender. So we just... mashed it with our hands. In a cup. It was basically just... sad juice.

jokes.text: That would be uncomfortable accepting free money. No, no, you'd be working. Doing her job, and I would pay you for it. Oh!
jokes.setup: Ted offers Alexis a job
jokes.punchline: Alexis misunderstanding the concept of employment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's privileged background

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: So I'd like to offer you a position at the firm. It would be part-time, flexible hours.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, yes! I would love a position. Like, what kind of position are we talking about? Because I'm very flexible, but I do have a bad back.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, I mean the job would have flexible hours. You'd be working in the office.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Right, okay, but like, would I have my own office? Because I need a space where I can really express myself, and honestly, fluorescent lighting is very aging.

jokes.text: That's the situation you never thought you'd be in?
jokes.setup: Johnny laments getting rides from his son
jokes.punchline: David's incredulous response to this specific complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect David reaction to his father's misplaced priorities

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I have to get rides from my own son.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, yes. That's what happens when you don't have a driver's license or a car.

jokes.text: Like when you were taking your harpsichord lessons, and I would drive you. I was seven.
jokes.setup: Johnny reminisces about driving David to lessons
jokes.punchline: David's deadpan age clarification
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the family's privileged past with David's practical response

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I remember driving you to all your lessons when you were a kid.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I was 11.

jokes.text: Well, believe it or not, this is actually not the most awkward parent/son request I've gotten today, so...
jokes.setup: Johnny asks David for money
jokes.punchline: David referencing the nude photo search earlier
jokes.explanation: Great callback connecting the episode's plotlines

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not giving you money. Do you know how many times I've had to Google myself to make sure there aren't any nude photos of me on the internet?

jokes.text: Well, I was trying to give you a hug. I-I get the gesture. I should've undone the seatbelt.
jokes.setup: Johnny tries to hug David in the car
jokes.punchline: Physical awkwardness of seatbelt hug attempt
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing father-son awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Come here, let me give you a hug.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, we're in a car.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: So? I can hug my son in a car.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: There's a seatbelt, and a steering wheel, and—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I don't care. Come here.

jokes.text: Well, you've seen me text.
jokes.setup: Ted asks about typing skills
jokes.punchline: Alexis equating texting with professional typing
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor showing Alexis's limited understanding of office skills

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Do you have any typing skills?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Um, I text all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That's not really the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I use my thumbs, so technically I'm typing with twice as many fingers as most people.

jokes.text: I'm loving this Q and A, but I think what's important right now, is my positive attitude. And I don't wanna brag, but 'Us Weekly' once described me as 'up for anything.'
jokes.setup: Alexis struggling in job interview
jokes.punchline: Her deflection to celebrity magazine quote
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis using celebrity culture as qualification, great character truth

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: So tell me about a time you demonstrated leadership.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Um, well, I was definitely a trendsetter. Like, I was the first person in my friend group to get a certain designer bag, and then everyone else got it. So I guess you could say I'm a leader?

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: That's... not really what we mean by leadership.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, as I told InStyle magazine in 2009, 'I go where fashion takes me.' And people really do follow my lead. So...

jokes.text: Yes, it's my character from 'Sunrise Bay,' but it's been put on the body of what I can only assume is an Indonesian lady boy.
jokes.setup: Finding photos online that appear to be Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's specific and problematic description
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's obliviousness but contains dated language that ages poorly

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I found several photos of myself online that I don't remember taking. One of them describes me as 'an aging glamour-pusher with a tenuous grasp on reality.'

jokes.text: That's your face. Yes, it's my character from 'Sunrise Bay,' but it's been put on the body of what I can only assume is an Indonesian lady boy.
jokes.setup: They find what appears to be nude photos of Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic description of the photo manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the photo search with Moira's characteristically dramatic assessment

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Are these... nude photos of you?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Those are not nudes, Stevie. They are artistic renderings of the female form. And yes, they are me, but they have been heavily photoshopped. My waist has been narrowed, my décolletage has been enhanced, and my skin has been smoothed to an airbrushed perfection that would make a Barbie doll weep with envy.

jokes.text: I thought maybe you just had a tan.
jokes.setup: Moira explains the photo manipulation
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry, innocent response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery, excellent economy

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I simply took a photo of myself, and used a photo editing application to enhance my features.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So you catfished yourself?

jokes.text: I am suddenly overwhelmed with regret. It's a new feeling for me, and I don't find it at all pleasurable.
jokes.setup: The photo search is ending unsuccessfully
jokes.punchline: Moira discovering regret for the first time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Moira experiencing a basic human emotion as a novel experience

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, this is depressing. I've searched through decades of photographs and I haven't found a single one where I look even remotely presentable.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Do you know what this means? I'm going to have to use a photo from the '80s. The '80s, David!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I think... I think I'm experiencing regret for the first time in my life.

jokes.text: And I should've appreciated those firm round mammae, and Callipygian ass, while I had them.
jokes.setup: Moira reflecting on lost nude photos
jokes.punchline: Her pretentious vocabulary for body parts
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira vocabulary pretension, perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I had the most exquisite décolletage in those photos. And my derrière was positively Rubenesque. The lighting really accentuated my... how do I put this delicately... my feminine landscape.

jokes.text: Take a thousand naked pictures of yourself now. You may currently think, 'oh, I'm too spooky.' Or, 'nobody wants to see these tiny boobies.'
jokes.setup: Moira giving Stevie life advice
jokes.punchline: Her specific self-deprecating examples
jokes.explanation: Great character moment mixing wisdom with inappropriate specificity

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You know, Stevie, I've learned that life is about embracing your flaws. I mean, look at me — I've had more failed business ventures than successful ones, my marriages have a higher turnover rate than a fast-casual restaurant, and my wigs alone could populate a small country. But do I let that stop me? Well, yes, frequently. But the point is, I'm still here, still fabulous, still wearing last season's Valentino like it's couture.

jokes.text: Otherwise, your own children will go looking for them one day and tragically, they won't be there.
jokes.setup: Moira continues her advice about nude photos
jokes.punchline: The implication that children searching for their parents' nude photos is normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Moira treating the episode's premise as universal experience

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The implication that children searching for their parents' nude photos is normal

jokes.text: It's actually just 'pin,' you don't have to say, 'pin number,' that's redundant.
jokes.setup: Johnny struggling with ATM
jokes.punchline: David's pedantic correction in crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect David being pedantic at worst possible moment

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I can't get any money out of this thing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's not a 'thing,' it's an automated teller machine.

jokes.text: Okay, can you just take a... take a big step! Thank you so much!
jokes.setup: ATM situation getting worse
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic thanks for Johnny backing away
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation showing father-son tension

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, thank you. Thank you so much for backing away. That really helped. I feel so much better now knowing that you're standing three feet away from me instead of four feet away.

jokes.text: He keeps saying pin number.
jokes.setup: Bank teller asks about the ATM problem
jokes.punchline: David's continued focus on the grammatical issue
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing David's inability to let go of the grammar issue

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, yes, it's broken. But more importantly, your sign says 'ATM Machine.' That's redundant. It's either an ATM or an automated teller machine. You don't say 'ATM Machine' any more than you'd say 'PIN number' or 'LCD display.' It's excessive and frankly, it's lazy.

jokes.text: Oh, which teller? Because it's only me and Carol here.
jokes.setup: Johnny claims he talked to a teller before
jokes.punchline: Teller's matter-of-fact response revealing the lie
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man comedy, efficient setup and payoff

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Rose
dialogue.line: I spoke with one of your tellers about this yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Bank Teller
dialogue.line: No, you didn't.

jokes.text: So if you could help me help him, that would be a wonderful thing.
jokes.setup: David explains the family's financial situation to the bank
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to make it sound noble
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing David trying to maintain dignity while begging

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We've lost everything. Our entire fortune. Gone. But we're not giving up. We're going to rebuild. From nothing. Which is actually... quite noble when you think about it. We're like pioneers. Except without the skills. Or the land. Or any idea what we're doing.

jokes.text: So if you could help me help him, that would be a wonderful thing.
jokes.setup: David trying to convince bank teller
jokes.punchline: His awkward emotional manipulation attempt
jokes.explanation: Shows David's discomfort with emotional expression

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm going to need you to do something for me. And I'm going to need you to do it without asking questions, without hesitation, and without making me feel like I'm asking for something unreasonable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I need you to process this transaction in a way that is... expedient. Because I have had a very difficult day, and I am not in the emotional space to deal with any sort of bureaucratic pushback.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You seem like a kind person. I can tell by the way you're looking at me with what I can only assume is pity. And I'm going to choose to believe that you are someone who helps people. So please, help me.

jokes.text: Sir, you just said your pin out loud. So I suggest you pick another one.
jokes.setup: David picks a new PIN
jokes.punchline: The obvious security problem with saying it aloud
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about basic security practices

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: My PIN is 1-2-3-4.

dialogue.speaker: Bank Teller
dialogue.line: Sir, you shouldn't say that out loud.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why not? It's my PIN.

dialogue.speaker: Bank Teller
dialogue.line: Because now everyone in the bank knows it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it's a good thing I have such a distinctive voice then.

jokes.text: Ted also proposed to you twice, so I'd say Ted's decision making skills are a little suspect.
jokes.setup: Alexis defending Ted's job offer
jokes.punchline: David's cutting observation about Ted's judgment
jokes.explanation: Good sibling burn with character history reference

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh, well if Ted's offering you a job, you should absolutely take it. A man who once thought a white fanny pack was appropriate business attire is really the gold standard for professional judgment.

jokes.text: He asked me several questions, most of which I answered.
jokes.setup: Alexis claims she went through a proper interview
jokes.punchline: Her admission that she didn't answer most questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - treating partial participation as complete success

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I went through a proper interview.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I didn't answer most of the questions.

jokes.text: My button! Things are looking up!
jokes.setup: Family in financial crisis
jokes.punchline: Johnny's excessive joy over finding lost button
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how low their standards have fallen

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God, oh my God! Do you know what this is? Do you have any idea what I'm holding right now?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A button?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Not just *a* button. This is the button from my Italian suit. The one I wore to the Gala in '94. I've been looking for this for twenty-three years!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you don't even own that suit anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's beside the point. This is a win, David. In a sea of losses, this is a win. I'm going to frame it.

jokes.text: My deepest condolences? I know things have been tough lately, but I'm here for you, and hope this helps. Love, Alexis.
jokes.setup: Alexis gives Johnny a card
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate condolence card as financial support
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis gesture - well-meaning but completely wrong

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I got you a card.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, thank you, sweetheart.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's a condolence card, but I wrote 'financial support' on the inside.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... that's very sweet, but also deeply depressing.

jokes.text: Was there supposed to be something in here? In where? In the card. What do you mean? Oh, it's just the card.
jokes.setup: Johnny opens what he thinks is an envelope with money
jokes.punchline: Alexis only gave him an empty sympathy card
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about Alexis's 'generous' gift, shows her good intentions but cluelessness

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What is this? There's nothing in here.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: It's a sympathy card.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A sympathy card? For what?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: For your financial situation.

jokes.text: David, Dad's poor right now! He's not poor anymore! Because someone gave him more than a cheap card!
jokes.setup: David doesn't want to hug Johnny
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic defense escalating to insulting her own gift
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Alexis accidentally undermining her own contribution

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, he's your father. You have to hug him.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't have to do anything.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, what is wrong with you? He's been nothing but supportive of you and your business.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: He literally bought you that store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm aware.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And he did it with love. Unlike my gift, which was clearly a mistake because it's a scarf from a store that no longer exists.

jokes.text: They say it's through our children that we stay young. But I haven't seen much effort on their part.
jokes.setup: Johnny praising their kids
jokes.punchline: Moira's typical self-centered complaint
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira making everything about herself with perfect delivery

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that's wonderful, Johnny. Though I do think you're overlooking the fact that I carried both of those children for nine months each, endured two very difficult deliveries, and have spent the better part of two decades trying to keep them from embarrassing themselves in public. So while I'm thrilled that you've noticed their accomplishments, I think we can all agree that my contribution has been somewhat more substantial.

jokes.text: Now how do I get this on the Internet? John!
jokes.setup: Moira finds old nude photo and appreciates it
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to post it online after earlier concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode conclusion - complete reversal of initial problem with classic Moira logic

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh my God, look at me. I was a vision. I need to post this immediately.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, didn't you just say you were concerned about your privacy online?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, but that was before I saw my body.

tmdb_overview: Moira is worried that racy photos from her past will jeopardize the campaign; Johnny asks for financial help; Alexis gets a job.
insight: Moira's vanity becomes the engine: 51 jokes in 42 minutes, mostly character-driven.
